texto: From History to ChanceThe river of time was flowing on its way, and I was swimming over its honey coloured surface with eyes closed. Does time move? It’s debatable. But we definitely move, age to age, with time and away from it, from its unmoving faces. To see its new faces. In its widest, longest and strangest art gallery.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jamaluddin Jamali que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “From History to ChanceThe river of time was flowing on its way, and I was swimming over its honey…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, romance-novels, faces, com foco em reflexao.

frase_original: From History to ChanceThe river of time was flowing on its way, and I was swimming over its honey coloured surface with eyes closed. Does time move? It’s debatable. But we definitely move, age to age, with time and away from it, from its unmoving faces. To see its new faces. In its widest, longest and strangest art gallery.

texto: Artmake me eternal, give such body to my undying soul. ask the art, art can do it......art can give you the age you want, the life span you want...art can give an ever lasting face to your love and joys; art can give an unending life to your acts, and an novel agelessness to your intercourse with this life that you love and we love...we all. Love, that too is Art and fill your memory yard with its art exhibitions....
explicacao: Citação de Jamaluddin Jamali sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Artmake me eternal, give such body to my undying soul. ask the art, art can do it......art can gi…” articula reflexao, love-story, artmake em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

texto: We all move on from history to chance, sorrow to sorrow, hope to hope, joy to joy. Read the book of fiction on the theme. its title is FROM HISTORY TO CHANCE
